source: Flightradar24 API reference tracks, transformed to DXB Unreal runway coordinates
note: Real reference track shapes are used as the map. Tracks are rotated per flight so the takeoff/landing tangent aligns to the supplied Unreal runway direction. Clean runway ground paths are added with small offsets. Smoothed version: airborne XY/Z points were low-pass smoothed to reduce zig-zag noise while preserving runway anchors, timings, IDs, and point counts. Runway direction fix: tracks were flipped 180 degrees around their assigned runway midpoint so the runway start/end direction is reversed while preserving timing, IDs, point counts, and smoothed track shapes. Arrival landing points were moved from the runway end to the first quarter / middle of each runway while preserving smoothed reference track shapes and timestamps. Departures mirrored across their assigned runway centerline; arrivals left unchanged.

arrival_landing_adjustment.description: Arrivals now touch down between roughly 24% and 46% from the runway start, then roll out only toward the middle, instead of landing at the runway end.

departure_mirror_adjustment.method: For each departure, XY points were reflected across the assigned runway line from runway start to endpoint. Timestamps, Z values, IDs, runway assignment, arrivals, and point counts were preserved.
